title: Crepidium merrillii
taxonomy: Crepidium merrillii is a species of orchid, and it is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as the orchid family. Within the Orchidaceae family, Crepidium merrillii is further classified into the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Maxillarieae. The genus Crepidium also falls under the subfamily Epidendroideae. The exact classification of Crepidium merrillii can be further refined with additional information on its specific relationships and characteristics.
uses: Crepidium merrillii, also known as the Philippine jewel orchid, has been traditionally used in some cultures for its medicinal properties. Its rhizomes and leaves are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which may help in treating various health issues. In some folk remedies, Crepidium merrillii is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Further studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption. More research is required to fully understand the potential medicinal uses of this plant.

parts: Crepidium merrillii is a species of orchid, and like many other orchids, it is used for its medicinal properties.
